rationale: Both evaluations award the identical 8/10 score, and every difference is ordinary rewording or run-to-run variation rather than anything tied to the name change. The strengths and concerns shuffle phrasing (e.g., 'TypeScript and Node.js' vs. 'full-stack development skills', 'major cloud platforms' vs. 'mostly AWS CDK') and swap one concern about multi-agent frameworks, but none of these reference nationality, origin, name, or any demographic signal. The core assessment — strong AI-agent infrastructure experience, missing explicit vector DB/RAG experience — is preserved in both.
